etymology.description: 沒(沒) (méi, "not have") indicates the negation, conveying the absence or lack of something. 問題(問題) (wèntí, "problem; question") consists of 問(問) (wèn, "ask") and 題(題) (tí, "topic; subject"), suggesting a topic that is inquired about. Together, 沒問題(沒問題) expresses that there is no problem or issue present.
etymology.interpretation: 沒(沒) (méi, "not have") directly negates the existence or presence of 問題(問題) (wèntí, "problem; question"), thus meaning 'no problem'. This phrase signifies reassurance and agreement, indicating that a situation is free from issues or complications. As problems are often topics of inquiry, 問題(問題) invokes the notion of things to ask about or resolve; hence 沒(沒) simply denies their presence, leading to the interpretation as 'no problem'.

examples.chinese: 如果你需要帮助(如果你需要幫助)，我会尽力(我會盡力)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ xūyào bāngzhù, wǒ huì jìnlì.
examples.english: If you need help, I will do my best.

examples.chinese: 请放心(請放心)，这个任务没问题(這個任務沒問題)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng fàngxīn, zhège rènwù méi wèntí.
examples.english: Rest assured, there's no problem with this task.

examples.chinese: 他答应了所有的工作要求(他答應了所有的工作要求)，所以领导很满意(所以領導很滿意)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dāyìngle suǒyǒu de gōngzuò yāoqiú, suǒyǐ lǐngdǎo hěn mǎnyì.
examples.english: He agreed to all the work requirements, so the leader was very satisfied.
